name: Cockroach Cove (Halona Cove)
tagline: The tiny hidden cove below the Halona Blowhole that most tourists photograph but never swim.
parking: Free lot at Halona Blowhole Lookout; short steep scramble down to the cove
bestTime: Summer (May–Sep) when south shore is calm; ONLY on flat days — waves funnel into this cove dangerously
talkStory: This tiny cove is the one from the movie From Here to Eternity. The snorkeling is surprisingly good in the protected pocket — clear water, reef fish, and sea urchins tucked into the lava rock. The catch is safety: waves wrap around both sides and can slam you into the rocks with zero warning. Only go on dead-calm days and keep one eye on the ocean at all times. The scramble down is steep and sketchy in slippers — wear proper shoes. If the Blowhole is actively spraying, the cove is too rough to enter.
